greeting: The magistrate weighs you like produce. "State your business or state your name. Either way, be brief. The wagon season waits for no man's troubles."
deflect: "You mistake me for a man with time."

entries.text: "Cresap. Magistrate. The name has been on this river longer than the town has, and it intends to stay."
"I keep the docket short and the wagons moving. Fines, writs, licenses. A town runs on paper, whatever the ROAD men tell you."
"Prospering, and it will keep prospering so long as nobody frightens the freight. Remember I said that."
"McTeague's stone pays half this town's wages. I will thank you not to repeat tavern talk about it in my hearing."

entries.deflect: "The court's records are the clerk's business, and the clerk keeps hours. So do I."
entries.text: His pen stops. "There are ledgers and ledgers, friend. The one you mean is not in this building, and if you know that much, then you and I should speak carefully, and not here."
